steps.title: A Signal in the Soil
steps.text: The habitat terminal flags a repeating sub-surface signal originating directly beneath your field. It pulses every time you harvest. Juno confirms: it is not geological.
steps.journal: A signal pulses beneath the farm, in time with my harvests. Ask around the outpost.

steps.title: The Botanist's Theory
steps.text: Sylla cross-references your codex scans. Every wild plant shares one genetic marker — a receiver. 'They're antennae,' she whispers. 'The whole biosphere is listening to something below.'
steps.journal: Sylla says the wild flora are antennae for something underground. Healthy soil strengthens the signal.

steps.title: A Chord Beneath the Field
steps.text: Your careful crop rotation has raised the field's resonance. The terminal now renders the signal as audio: a slow, vast chord — the sound of something enormous, dreaming.
steps.journal: The signal is a chord. The healthier my soil, the clearer it sings. Keep rotating crops; keep harvesting.

steps.title: What the Caretaker Heard
steps.text: CARE-7 opens a sealed log: the previous homesteader heard the chord too — and fled. 'DIRECTIVE CONFLICT,' the unit hums. 'They feared the planet. This unit believes the planet was only lonely. Go to the great crystal. At night. It waits.'
steps.journal: CARE-7: the last farmer fled the voice. Visit the great crystal east of the field — at night.

steps.title: The Dreaming Ground
steps.text: You touch the great crystal under the twin moons. Light floods the field — every furrow you tilled glows like a circuit. A voice, made of root and mineral and patience: 'YOU TEND. WE WAKE. GROW WITH US.' Something small and warm settles into your pocket: a seed that did not exist yesterday.
steps.journal: The planet spoke. It gave me a seed — a Dream Lotus. Veridia is awake, and we are its garden.
